dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: Something the design work actually produced — a token set, a user flow, a component diagram, a wireframe, or brand guidelines. The design strategy says what the design should be; an asset is the thing itself.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.description: The Product this asset belongs to

entries.rules: one of: tokens / uiFlow / componentDiagram / wireframe / brandGuidelines
entries.description: What sort of artefact this is. The kind decides the format — see below
What this artefact is called. Regenerating the same name replaces it rather than leaving two

entries.rules: must parse as JSON when the kind is tokens; must start with a Mermaid diagram type when the kind is uiFlow or componentDiagram
one of: json / mermaid / markdown; decided by the kind, never by the caller
entries.description: How the content should be read
Set once this asset has been pushed into a Figma file
Where in that file it landed, when known
When the asset was first stored

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Until the asset or its Product is deleted. Regenerating an asset of the same name replaces it in place.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?
sensitiveFields.answer: No. The Figma token that pushes these lives in the operating system's credential store and never touches this table.

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: The kind decides the format, not whoever called save. A token set must parse as JSON and a flow must start with a Mermaid diagram type, because everything downstream — the emitted file, the Figma push — assumes it. Recording where an asset landed in Figma never changes the asset's content: pushing does not modify the thing, it records that a copy exists elsewhere.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: New: the Marketing & Design area needs somewhere to keep what design produces, so the app can show it, export it as files, and know which artefacts have been pushed to Figma.
